summary: WebGL Performance: Massive Data Rendering Optimization Planning
Viewport Culling Planning & Trackpad Zoom Bug

message.content: Olá Claude! Por favor, verifique o hasness descrito no CLAUDE.md e continue com a próxima tarefa sempre seguindo o Harness Protocol - ignore o  contexto e arquivos na pasta ./packages, eles são tratados por outra equipe. Execute o fluxo seguindo sempre o harness e comitando adequadamente até a finalizacao e testes de todas as tarefas.

content.thinking:
O usuário está pedindo para eu seguir o protocolo do Harness descrito no CLAUDE.md. Vou começar lendo os arquivos de estado do harness para entender o contexto atual e a próxima tarefa.

De acordo com o protocolo:
1. READ claude-progress.txt → histórico da sessão
2. READ features.json → próxima feature (passes:false)
3. IMPLEMENT uma feature de cada vez
4. TEST visualmente com Playwright MCP
5. COMMIT após feature passar
6. UPDATE claude-progress.txt

Vou começar lendo os dois arquivos principais do harness.

content.text: Olá! Vou seguir o Harness Protocol. Primeiro, preciso ler os arquivos de estado.

   853→    },
   854→    {
   855→      "id": "BUG-009",
   856→      "category": "performance",
   857→      "priority": 0,
   858→      "description": "Trackpad zoom performance issue on MacBook - lag/stutter when zooming via pinch gesture",
   859→      "passes": false,

   861→      "investigation": {

   868→          "wheelAccumulatorRef.current.timeout clears/resets too frequently",
   869→          "F121 trackpad detection (20ms threshold) may not be tuned for Magic Trackpad"
   870→        ],

   875→          "Constants: ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION=250ms, ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT=50ms, TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY=0.001"
   876→        ],
   877→        "proposedFix": {

   879→          "option2_targetUpdate": "During animation, update target instead of starting new animation",

   881→        }
   882→      },
   883→      "notes": "Reported 2025-12-17. User confirms minimap navigation is smooth, only trackpad zoom has issues. MacBook Pro Magic Trackpad is the test device."
   884→    }

   886→  "categories": {

   899→  },

{
  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",
  "version": "0.3.1",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-17",
  "product": {
    "entry": "apps/canvas",
    "description": "Production React app with semantic zoom (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6)"
  },
  "reference": {
    "entry": "design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
    "description": "Standalone POC for visual reference only"
  },
  "priorityQueue": {
    "description": "Features ordered by execution priority. Execute in order, respecting blockedBy dependencies.",
    "note": "Priority 0 = immediate, Priority 1 = high, Priority 2 = after dependencies"
  },
  "parallelExecution": {
    "description": "Parallel execution configuration for remaining features (updated 2025-12-17)",
    "runner": "bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts",
    "worktreeDir": ".trees/",
    "status": "MOSTLY COMPLETE - Only 6 features remain",
    "waves": [
      {
        "wave": 1,
        "description": "Navigation enhancements + Task panel (can run in parallel)",
        "maxConcurrency": 3,
        "groups": [
          {"id": "group-A", "features": ["F016"], "sequential": false, "files": ["InfiniteCanvas.tsx"], "note": "Shift+Scroll vertical pan"},
          {"id": "group-B", "features": ["F026"], "sequential": false, "files": ["App.tsx"], "note": "Task details panel"},
          {"id": "group-C", "features": ["F027", "F028"], "sequential": true, "files": ["InfiniteCanvas.tsx"], "note": "Keyboard shortcuts zoom/pan"}
        ]
      },
      {
        "wave": 2,
        "description": "Accessibility (depends on Wave 1)",
        "maxConcurrency": 1,
        "dependsOn": 1,
        "groups": [
          {"id": "group-D", "features": ["F021"], "sequential": false, "files": ["InfiniteCanvas.tsx", "layers"], "note": "Keyboard task navigation"}
        ]
      },
      {
        "wave": 3,
        "description": "Final accessibility (depends on F021)",
        "maxConcurrency": 1,
        "dependsOn": 2,
        "groups": [
          {"id": "group-E", "features": ["F022"], "sequential": false, "files": ["InfiniteCanvas.tsx", "MicroLayer.tsx"], "note": "ARIA labels"}
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "PERF-002",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Viewport Culling & Spatial Indexing - Only render/process entities visible in viewport",
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "research": "docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md",
      "rationale": "Current InstancedMesh sends all 60k instances to GPU regardless of viewport. This wastes GPU bandwidth and CPU cycles. Implementing viewport culling with R-tree spatial index can reduce rendered instances from 60k to ~500 (visible only), dramatically improving performance on mobile devices.",
      "relatedBugs": ["BUG-009"],
      "implementationPlan": {
        "phase0_trackpad_zoom_fix": {
          "description": "Fix trackpad zoom jank before spatial optimizations (BUG-009)",
          "rationale": "User reported smooth minimap navigation but laggy trackpad zoom. This is an input event handling issue, NOT a rendering issue. Must be fixed first as it affects perceived performance regardless of culling.",
          "tasks": [
            "Profile trackpad zoom events in Chrome DevTools (Performance tab)",
            "Identify if bottleneck is animation (250ms) or accumulation timeout (50ms)",
            "Option A: Remove animation for trackpad - apply zoom directly like minimap",
            "Option B: Update target during animation instead of starting new animation",
            "Option C: Replace custom implementation with d3-zoom or @use-gesture/react",
            "Test on MacBook Pro Magic Trackpad specifically",
            "Ensure smooth 60fps during rapid pinch-to-zoom gestures"
          ],
          "marketSolutions": {
            "d3-zoom": "Industry standard, handles wheel/touch normalization, momentum",
            "use-gesture": "@use-gesture/react - React hooks for gestures, used by react-three/drei",
            "leva-zoom": "Study how leva/drei implement zoom for R3F"
          }
        },
        "phase1_spatial_interface": {
          "description": "Define spatial query INTERFACE (implementation deferred to STATE-ARCH-001)",
          "rationale": "STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 implements full RBush with TypedArrays. Here we only define the interface and use a simple O(n) filter as placeholder. This avoids duplicate work.",
          "tasks": [
            "Create ISpatialIndex interface at apps/canvas/src/spatial/types.ts",
            "Interface: getVisibleTasks(viewportBounds) → taskIds[]",
            "Create SimpleSpatialIndex with O(n) filter (temporary placeholder)",
            "SimpleSpatialIndex uses existing object-based task array",
            "STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 will replace with RBush + TypedArrays"
          ],
          "deferredTo": "STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 - Full RBush implementation with dirty flagging"
        },
        "phase2_culled_instanced_mesh": {
          "description": "Implement per-instance culling for InstancedMesh",
          "tasks": [
            "Create CulledInstancedMesh wrapper at apps/canvas/src/rendering/CulledInstancedMesh.ts",
            "Implement instance reordering: visible instances at front, hidden at back",
            "Use mesh.count to render only visible instances (single draw call)",
            "Track dirty regions for partial buffer updates (avoid full re-upload)",
            "Pre-allocate reorder buffers to avoid GC in hot path",
            "Benchmark: Update time should be <1ms for 1000 visible instances"
          ]
        },
        "phase3_layer_integration": {
          "description": "Integrate culling with existing LOD layers",
          "tasks": [
            "Update MesoLayer to use CulledInstancedMesh + SpatialIndex",
            "Update MacroLayer to use viewport-based heatmap aggregation",
            "Verify MicroLayer already uses filterVisibleTasks (improve with R-tree)",
            "Add viewportBounds calculation in InfiniteCanvas",
            "Pass viewportBounds to all layers",
            "Ensure culling works during pan/zoom animations"
          ]
        },
        "phase4_validation": {
          "description": "Performance validation and profiling",
          "tasks": [
            "Create performance test suite with __PERF_START_SESSION__",
            "Measure FPS during rapid pan/zoom with all 60k tasks",
            "Profile with Chrome DevTools - verify reduced GPU work",
            "Test on mobile devices (iOS Safari, Chrome Android)",
            "Document before/after metrics in PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md"
          ]
        }
      },
      "performanceTargets": {
        "viewportQuery": "<5ms for 1000 results",
        "instanceUpdate": "<1ms for 1000 visible",
        "fps": "60fps constant during pan/zoom",
        "gpuInstances": "Only visible count (not all 60k)"
      },
      "libraries": {
        "spatialIndex": "RBush (R-tree) - https://github.com/mourner/rbush",
        "alternative": "Flatbush (static, 5-8x faster if data rarely changes)"
      },
      "notes": "PENDING - Incremental optimization BEFORE STATE-ARCH-001. Phase 0 (BUG-009) fixes trackpad zoom. Phase 1 creates ISpatialIndex INTERFACE only (placeholder implementation). STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 implements full RBush with TypedArrays. CulledInstancedMesh (Phase 2-3) prepares rendering for STATE-ARCH-001. Research: docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md. 2025-12-17."
    },
    {
      "id": "STATE-ARCH-001",
      "category": "architecture",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "Refatorar arquitetura de state management para padrão Dual-Store de alta performance",
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "PERF-002",
      "research": "docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf",
      "rationale": "Documento de pesquisa identifica GC como gargalo principal em aplicações WebGL móveis. React Hooks e stores reativos são inadequados para updates a 60fps. Propõe arquitetura Dual-Store separando Simulation State (TypedArrays/ECS) de Application State (@mms/toolkit state-machine). NOTA: Usamos @mms/toolkit (packages/toolkit) para UI state, NUNCA Zustand.",
      "implementationPlan": {
        "phase1_simulation_store": {
          "description": "Criar Simulation State Store (TypedArrays) - SEPARADO de UI State",
          "tasks": [
            "Criar EntityStore baseado em TypedArrays com padrão Structure of Arrays (SoA)",
            "Implementar Float32Array para posições (startDay, duration) e Uint8Array para flags (hasConflict, type)",
            "Criar Views/Cursors para acesso ergonômico aos dados tipados",
            "Acesso DIRETO via índices - sem reatividade, sem proxies, sem overhead",
            "Benchmark: 100k entidades devem ocupar ~800KB vs dezenas de MB com objetos JS"
          ],
          "clarification": "Este store é para SIMULATION STATE (dados de entidades a 60fps). NÃO usa @mms/toolkit. Acesso direto, mutação direta, máxima performance."
        },
        "phase2_spatial_index": {
          "description": "Implementar indexação espacial com R-tree (substitui placeholder de PERF-002)",
          "prerequisite": "PERF-002.Phase1 defines ISpatialIndex interface. This phase implements full RBush backing.",
          "tasks": [
            "Instalar RBush para R-tree (bun add rbush @types/rbush)",
            "Implementar RBushSpatialIndex que satisfaz ISpatialIndex (de PERF-002)",
            "Adaptar para trabalhar com TypedArrays do Phase1 (não objetos JS)",
            "Otimizar: bulk loading em startup, rebuild apenas em create/delete (não em move)",
            "Implementar dirty flagging para minimizar recálculos",
            "Substituir SimpleSpatialIndex (placeholder PERF-002) por RBushSpatialIndex",
            "Target: queries de viewport com 1000 resultados em <5ms"
          ]
        },
        "phase3_ui_state_bridge": {
          "description": "UI State via @mms/toolkit + Bridge com Simulation State",
          "tasks": [
            "@mms/toolkit é EXCLUSIVAMENTE para UI State (selectedId, hoveredId, panelOpen, tool)",
            "Criar slice 'interaction' com selectedEntityId, hoveredEntityId",
            "Criar slice 'ui' com toolActive, panelsOpen, zoomLevel para display",
            "Bridge: Scheduler sincroniza Simulation→UI a 30fps máximo (throttled)",
            "UI NUNCA acessa TypedArrays diretamente - apenas via bridge/selectors"
          ],
          "clarification": "@mms/toolkit gerencia APENAS estado de interface (event-driven, <30fps). Simulation State (60fps, TypedArrays) é separado e acessado via bridge throttled."
        },
        "phase4_sync_loop_scheduler": {
          "description": "Implementar Sync Loop centralizado (ordem fixa de execução)",
          "tasks": [
            "Criar scheduler com ordem fixa: processInput() → updateSimulation() → renderCanvas() → syncUI()",
            "updateSimulation opera em TypedArrays (Simulation State)",
            "syncUI opera em @mms/toolkit (UI State) - throttled a 30fps",
            "Desacoplar physics/simulation tick de render tick para determinismo",
            "requestAnimationFrame como driver principal do loop"
          ]
        },
        "phase5_object_pooling_gc_elimination": {
          "description": "Eliminar pressão de GC no hot path",
          "tasks": [
            "Criar VectorPool para Vector3/Vector2 temporários no render loop",
            "Criar ResultPool para arrays de resultados de queries",
            "REGRA: Zero 'new Object()' no requestAnimationFrame loop",
            "REGRA: Evitar closures em funções chamadas por frame (closures capturam escopo)",
            "REGRA: Preferir 'for' loops sobre forEach/map/filter (criam contexto de função)",
            "REGRA: Pré-computar valores constantes FORA do loop de renderização",
            "Validar via Chrome DevTools Memory tab - zero GC spikes durante pan/zoom"
          ]
        },
        "phase6_mobile_optimizations": {
          "description": "Otimizações específicas para navegadores móveis",
          "tasks": [
            "Passive event listeners: { passive: true } em todos touch/wheel handlers",
            "Apenas marcar { passive: false } quando preventDefault() é necessário",
            "Monitorar memória via performance.memory (Chrome) ou estimativa de ArrayBuffer sizes",
            "Implementar LOD mais agressivo para tiles fora do centro do viewport",
            "Respeitar limites iOS Safari: 256MB canvas, ~380MB WebGL total",
            "Testar em dispositivos móveis reais (não apenas emulador)"
          ]
        },
        "phase7_undo_redo": {
          "description": "Implementar sistema de Undo/Redo",
          "tasks": [
            "Implementar Command Pattern para operações estruturais (create/delete/group)",
            "Usar Immer patches para mudanças de propriedade (move, resize) - Immer já é dependência",
            "Limite de histórico: 100-200 entradas para controlar memória",
            "Pausar histórico durante drag operations (agrupar micro-mudanças em 1 undo)",
            "Serialização binária de deltas para histórico compacto"
          ]
        },
        "phase8_worker_layer_optional": {
          "description": "[OPCIONAL] Worker Layer para offload de processamento",
          "tasks": [
            "OffscreenCanvas para renderização em thread separada (se suportado)",
            "SharedArrayBuffer para compartilhamento zero-copy entre main e worker",
            "Offload de cálculos pesados (pathfinding, collision detection) para Web Workers",
            "NOTA: Requer headers COOP/COEP para SharedArrayBuffer"
          ],
          "priority": "low - implementar apenas se necessário após profiling"
        }
      },
      "performanceTargets": {
        "entities": "100.000+ tasks renderizáveis",
        "fps": "60fps constante durante pan/zoom",
        "memory": "<100MB para 100k entidades",
        "gcPauses": "Zero pausas perceptíveis durante interação",
        "queryTime": "<5ms para viewport queries"
      },
      "libraries": {
        "entityStore": "Custom TypedArray-based (Structure of Arrays pattern)",
        "spatialIndex": "RBush (R-tree)",
        "uiState": "@mms/toolkit state-machine (packages/toolkit) - NUNCA Zustand",
        "uiBridge": "@mms/toolkit useSelector + useSyncExternalStore (já implementado)",
        "undoRedo": "Command Pattern + Immer patches (Immer já é dependência do toolkit)",
        "ecs": "bitECS (opcional, se complexidade justificar)"
      },
      "notes": "PENDING - Task criada baseada em docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf. Documento analisa arquiteturas de Figma, Google Maps, CesiumJS e motores de jogos WebGL. Recomenda Dual-Store: Simulation State (TypedArrays, 60fps) separado de Application State (event-driven). IMPORTANTE: Para UI state usamos EXCLUSIVAMENTE @mms/toolkit (packages/toolkit), NUNCA Zustand como sugere o PDF. O toolkit já usa Immer e useSyncExternalStore. Implementação incremental: EntityStore → spatial index → UI bridge → undo/redo. 2025-12-17."
    },
    {
      "id": "URGENT-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Auditar e documentar uso de React hooks - migrar apenas estados críticos para external stores",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE (REAVALIADO) - Audit done: cameraPosRef workaround resolves critical stale closure bug (F122). Other states (isInitialized, canvasSize, isPanning, panStart) are UI-only or one-time - no issues. useRef for momentum/animation is correct pattern. Added React hooks vs external stores guidelines to CLAUDE.md. Full migration to external stores would be over-engineering. If new stale closure bugs appear, migrate case by case. 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "PERF-001",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Adicionar ferramenta de desenvolvimento para métricas de performance WebGL em tempo real + logging detalhado",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented r3f-perf 7.2.3 with PerfMonitor component. Features: visual panel (FPS/MS/MB/GPU/draw calls/triangles), logging system with circular buffer (60s), session recording API (START_SESSION/STOP_SESSION). Toggle via 'P' key or navbar button. Global API: window.__PERF_LOG__, __PERF_EXPORT__, __PERF_START_SESSION__, __PERF_STOP_SESSION__, __PERF_CLEAR__. Known issue: r3f-perf has expandShaderIncludes console spam due to troika-three-utils compatibility with Three.js 0.170+ (tracked in r3f-perf#58). Does not affect functionality. Documented in docs/PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-003",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "Atualizar protocolo de testes visuais do Harness para incluir métricas de performance (visual + logging)",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "PERF-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Updated docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md with comprehensive performance testing section. Added: Quick Check (visual) and Detailed Check (logging) approaches, Playwright integration examples, approval criteria matrix, when to use each approach guide, known issues section. Updated testingProtocol in features.json with performance criteria and mandatory step 5. Performance testing is now MANDATORY for all features. 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-004",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "Migrar tarefas POC do archive para fluxo normal de features pendentes",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Migrated 12 POC features (F016-F023, F025-F028) from archive to active features list. Removed -POC suffix, assigned priorities (2-4 based on importance), set proper blockedBy dependencies. F024-POC archived (covered by F116). Archive now only contains F117 and F024-POC. Added migrationNote to archive section. 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Configure Playwright MCP for headless without focus stealing",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Playwright MCP configured with --headless flag in .mcp.json. Deny permissions added to block access to personal directories."
    },
    {
      "id": "QA-PLAN-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Plan QA testing protocol with deep thinking (planning mode)",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "HARNESS-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Created docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md with WebGL testing protocol. Updated session-start.sh and end-of-turn-check.sh hooks. Protocol includes: typecheck, visual test, console check, interaction test, performance check sequence."
    },
    {
      "id": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "category": "research",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "UX Best Practices Deep Research & Documentation",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "HARNESS-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Created docs/UX_RESEARCH.md with comprehensive research on: Figma/Google Maps/iOS patterns, mouse/touch/pointer events, wheel normalization (Facebook pattern), momentum physics, cross-platform scroll differences, accessibility requirements. Includes implementation recommendations for F118-F121."
    },
    {
      "id": "F118",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Momentum/inertia panning (velocity-based drag)",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented with velocity tracking during drag, friction-based animation loop (0.93 friction), and momentum stops at velocity < 0.5. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F119",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Smooth zoom UX (iOS/Google Maps feel)",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented quadOut easing for zoom animations (250ms), wheel event accumulation for trackpad smoothing, pinch-to-zoom support via Pointer Events API, animated zoom for +/- buttons and Reset. Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F120",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Double-click to zoom in (BUG FIX)",
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE - Implemented with 350ms cubicOut animation. Zooms 2x toward click point, respects MAX_ZOOM (5.0x). Tested via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F121",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Cross-platform scroll/input sensitivity normalization",

        "suspectedCauses": [
          "ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION (250ms) creates queued animations during rapid pinch",
          "ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT (50ms) delays execution causing perceived lag",
          "animateZoomTo() starts new requestAnimationFrame per zoom event instead of updating target",
          "wheelAccumulatorRef.current.timeout clears/resets too frequently",
          "F121 trackpad detection (20ms threshold) may not be tuned for Magic Trackpad"
        ],
        "relevantCode": [
          "InfiniteCanvas.tsx:handleWheel (lines ~1191-1256)",
          "InfiniteCanvas.tsx:executeAccumulatedZoom (lines ~1076-1127)",
          "InfiniteCanvas.tsx:animateZoomTo (lines ~881-952)",
          "Constants: ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION=250ms, ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT=50ms, TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY=0.001"
        ],
        "proposedFix": {
          "option1_immediateZoom": "Remove animation for trackpad zoom - apply directly like minimap does",
          "option2_targetUpdate": "During animation, update target instead of starting new animation",
          "option3_gestureLerp": "Use d3-zoom or use-gesture library for production-grade pinch handling"
        }

    50→## Session: 2025-12-17 (PERF-002 + BUG-009 Planning) - COMPLETE ✅
    51→
    52→### PERF-002: Viewport Culling & Spatial Indexing - PLANNED
    53→### BUG-009: Trackpad Zoom Performance - IDENTIFIED
    54→
    55→**Objective:**
    56→Deep research and planning for viewport culling optimization + Investigation of trackpad zoom performance issue on MacBook.
    57→
    58→**Research Conducted:**
    59→- Web searches: Three.js frustum culling, R3F viewport culling, RBush spatial indexing, deck.gl massive rendering, Figma WebGL architecture
    60→- Read: `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf` (11 pages)
    61→- Read: `docs/RESEARCH.md` (railway scheduling - not relevant)
    62→- Read: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (current LOD implementation)
    63→
    64→**Key Findings:**
    65→
    66→1. **InstancedMesh Problem:** Three.js sends ALL instances to GPU regardless of viewport
    67→   - 60k instances = 60k transforms uploaded every frame
    68→   - No built-in per-instance frustum culling
    69→
    70→2. **RBush Spatial Index:**
    71→   - Bulk load 1M items ~1600ms (one-time)
    72→   - Viewport query for 1000 results ~4ms
    73→   - Perfect for viewport culling queries
    74→
    75→3. **Culling Strategy:**
    76→   - Reorder visible instances to front of buffer
    77→   - Set `mesh.count = visibleCount` (only visible rendered)
    78→   - No new allocations during pan/zoom
    79→
    80→4. **Alternative Libraries:**
    81→   - `@three.ez/instanced-mesh` (InstancedMesh2) - has BVH + per-instance culling
    82→   - `three-mesh-bvh` - accelerated raycasting
    83→   - `flatbush` - 5-8x faster than RBush for static data
    84→
    85→**Documents Created:**
    86→- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` - Comprehensive research consolidating all findings
    87→
    88→**Task Created: PERF-002**
    89→
    90→Added to features.json with 4 implementation phases:
    91→
    92→1. **Phase 1 - Spatial Index:** RBush R-tree for task/entity lookup
    93→2. **Phase 2 - Culled InstancedMesh:** Instance reordering + count manipulation
    94→3. **Phase 3 - Layer Integration:** MesoLayer, MacroLayer viewport-aware
    95→4. **Phase 4 - Validation:** Performance testing and profiling
    96→
    97→**Dependency Update:**
    98→- STATE-ARCH-001 now `blockedBy: "PERF-002"`
    99→- PERF-002 is incremental improvement that can be done BEFORE full architecture migration
   100→- Establishes viewport culling foundation that STATE-ARCH-001 will build upon
   101→
   102→**Performance Targets:**
   103→- Viewport query: <5ms for 1000 results
   104→- Instance update: <1ms for 1000 visible
   105→- FPS: 60fps constant during pan/zoom
   106→- GPU instances: Only visible count (not all 60k)
   107→
   108→**BUG-009 Investigation (Added):**
   109→- User reported: Trackpad zoom has lag/stutter, but minimap navigation is smooth
   110→- Diagnosis: This is INPUT HANDLING issue, not rendering (minimap proves rendering is fine)
   111→- Root cause candidates:
   112→  - ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION=250ms creates queued animations
   113→  - ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT=50ms delays execution
   114→  - animateZoomTo() starts new RAF instead of updating target
   115→- Solution options:
   116→  - Option A: Remove animation for trackpad (direct zoom like minimap)
   117→  - Option B: Update target during animation
   118→  - Option C: Use d3-zoom or @use-gesture/react (market solutions)
   119→
   120→**Files Changed:**

   130→- Fix: PERF-002.Phase1 now defines ISpatialIndex INTERFACE only + placeholder
   131→- STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 implements full RBush with TypedArrays
   132→- No more duplicate work - tasks are now complementary

   522→2. **Terminal session spawning** - Opens separate Claude sessions per feature
   523→3. **Isolation prompts** - Each session restricted to its feature scope

   543→# Merge completed feature

   697→## Session: 2025-12-17 (TECH-DEBT-001 Implementation) - COMPLETE ✅
   698→
   699→### TECH-DEBT-001: Migrate Camera State to @mms/toolkit State Machine - COMPLETE
   700→
   701→**Objective:**
   702→Replace React useState/useRef with @mms/toolkit external store to solve stale closure problems in animation loops.
   703→
   704→**Implementation:**
   705→
   706→1. **Created viewport slice (`apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts`):**
   707→   - ViewportSliceState interface with camera, canvasSize, momentum, animation, pan states
   708→   - createSyncSlice with all reducers: setCamera, setMomentum, startAnimation, startPan, etc.
   709→   - Cache policy: 'session' (resets when app closes)
   710→
   711→2. **Created canvas machine (`apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts`):**
   712→   - createMachine with viewport slice
   713→   - Exports: useCanvas, useCanvasSelector, canvasActions, canvasStore
   714→   - Context with userID: null (anonymous canvas)
   715→
   716→3. **Migrated InfiniteCanvas.tsx:**
   717→   - Replaced useState calls with useCanvasSelector for reactive state
   718→   - Animation loops use canvasStore.getState() for synchronous access (no stale closures)
   719→   - Pan/zoom handlers use canvasStore.dispatch(canvasActions.viewport.xxx())
   720→   - Removed cameraPosRef workaround (no longer needed with external store)
   721→
   722→**Errors Fixed:**
   723→- `cache: { strategy: 'none' }` → `cache: { policy: 'session' }` (toolkit requires 'policy' field)
   724→- `context: {}` → `context: { userID: null }` (toolkit requires userID in context)
   725→
   726→**Files Created:**
   727→- `apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts` (~235 lines)
   728→- `apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts` (~55 lines)
   729→- `apps/canvas/src/state/index.ts` (re-exports)
   730→
   731→**Files Modified:**

   906→### PERF-001: Performance Monitor with Logging System - COMPLETE

   919→   - PerfLogger sub-component tracks metrics every frame
   920→   - Circular buffer stores last 60s of performance data

   924→   - `window.__PERF_LOG__` - Access log buffer (last 60s)

   926→   - `window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name)` - Start recording session
   927→   - `window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__()` - Stop and get aggregated stats
   928→   - `window.__PERF_SESSION_STATUS__()` - Check active session
   929→   - `window.__PERF_CLEAR__()` - Clear log buffer

   932→   - FPS: avg, min, max, p1 (worst 1%), p99 (best 99%)
   933→   - MS: avg, max frame time

   969→### Novas Tarefas Adicionadas ao Harness
   970→
   971→**Contexto:**

   974→**Tarefas Criadas:**

   976→1. **URGENT-001** (Priority 0 - EMERGENCIAL)
   977→   - Migrar uso de React hooks para external stores em todo o projeto
   978→   - React hooks causam problemas em WebGL: stale closures, batching, re-renders
   979→   - External stores (useSyncExternalStore) resolvem todos esses problemas
   980→   - Auditar código, documentar usos, migrar estados críticos
   981→   - Atualizar CLAUDE.md com regra contra React hooks para estado crítico
   982→
   983→2. **PERF-001** (Priority 0)
   984→   - Adicionar ferramenta de métricas de performance WebGL em tempo real
   985→   - Ferramentas pesquisadas: r3f-perf (recomendada), stats-gl, stats.js
   986→   - Painel deve mostrar: FPS, MS, MB, GPU time, draw calls, triangles
   987→   - Visível para AI (via screenshot) e humanos (no browser)
   988→   - Toggle para mostrar/esconder (tecla 'P' ou botão)
   989→   - **LOGGING DETALHADO** (adicionado após revisão):
   990→     - Buffer circular em memória para últimos 60s de métricas
   991→     - window.__PERF_LOG__ para acessar log via console
   992→     - window.__PERF_EXPORT__() para exportar log completo
   993→     - window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name) / window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__()
   994→     - Estatísticas agregadas: FPS médio, p1, p99, frames abaixo de 30/60fps
   995→     - AI pode usar browser_evaluate para análise programática

   998→3. **HARNESS-003** (Priority 1, blockedBy: PERF-001)
   999→   - Atualizar protocolo de testes visuais para incluir métricas de performance
  1000→   - Métricas obrigatórias: FPS >= 60 idle, >= 30 interação; MS <= 16.67ms
  1001→   - **DUAS abordagens complementares**:
  1002→     - VISUAL: ler métricas do painel no screenshot (quick check)

  1142→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F122 - Fix elastic/jump drag bug) - COMPLETE ✅
  1143→
  1144→### F122: Fix elastic/jump effect when dragging - COMPLETE
  1145→
  1146→**Problem:**
  1147→Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.
  1148→
  1149→**Root Cause Analysis:**
  1150→React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:
  1151→1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state
  1152→2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position
  1153→3. New drag starts from stale position → canvas "jumps" back
  1154→
  1155→**Solution Implemented:**
  1156→1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef
  1157→2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state
  1158→3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:
  1159→   - `handleCameraInitialized`
  1160→   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)
  1161→   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)
  1162→   - `handlePinchZoom`
  1163→   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)
  1164→   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)
  1165→4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state
  1166→
  1167→**Files Changed:**
  1168→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)
  1169→- `features.json` (F122 passes: true)

  1197→### F120: Double-click to zoom in - COMPLETE
  1198→
  1199→**Objective:**
  1200→Implement double-click to zoom in 2x centered on click location.
  1201→
  1202→**Implementation:**
  1203→- Already implemented in F119 (smooth zoom) session
  1204→- `handleDoubleClick` function in InfiniteCanvas.tsx (lines 1009-1074)
  1205→- Zooms 2x toward click point with 350ms cubicOut animation
  1206→- Respects MAX_ZOOM (5.0x) limit
  1207→
  1208→**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
  1209→- ✅ Double-click at 1.00x → 2.00x (2x zoom)
  1210→- ✅ Double-click at 2.00x → 4.00x (2x zoom)
  1211→- ✅ Double-click at 4.00x → 5.00x (clamped to MAX_ZOOM)
  1212→- ✅ Double-click at 5.00x stays at 5.00x (respects limit)
  1213→- ✅ Smooth animation with cubicOut easing
  1214→- ✅ No console errors
  1215→
  1216→### F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE
  1217→
  1218→**Objective:**
  1219→Normalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.
  1220→
  1221→**Implementation:**
  1222→
  1223→1. **deltaMode Normalization:**
  1224→   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)
  1225→   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)
  1226→   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly
  1227→
  1228→2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**
  1229→   - Track event frequency with timestamps
  1230→   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior
  1231→   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad
  1232→   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode
  1233→
  1234→3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**
  1235→   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003
  1236→   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)
  1237→
  1238→4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**
  1239→   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5
  1240→   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events
  1241→
  1242→**Constants Added:**
  1243→```typescript
  1244→LINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line
  1245→PAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page
  1246→MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity
  1247→TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad
  1248→MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event
  1249→TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events
  1250→TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm
  1251→```
  1252→
  1253→**Files Changed:**
  1254→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (normalizeWheelDelta function, updated handleWheel)
  1255→- `features.json` (F120, F121 passes: true)

  1259→- ✅ Scroll up (deltaY=-500) zooms in: 0.61x → 1.00x
  1260→- ✅ No console errors
  1261→- ✅ Smooth zoom animation
  1262→
  1263→**Next:** F116 (Performance), F117 (Visual parity)

  1266→
  1267→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE ✅
  1268→
  1269→### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE
  1270→
  1271→**Objective:**
  1272→Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.
  1273→
  1274→**Implementation:**
  1275→
  1276→1. **Velocity Tracking:**
  1277→   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps
  1278→   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation
  1279→   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity
  1280→
  1281→2. **Momentum Animation:**
  1282→   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples
  1283→   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity
  1284→   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
  1285→   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units
  1286→
  1287→3. **Integration:**
  1288→   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
  1289→   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
  1290→   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation
  1291→
  1292→**Constants Used:**
  1293→```typescript
  1294→MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier
  1295→MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
  1296→MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average
  1297→```
  1298→
  1299→**Files Changed:**
  1300→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)
  1301→- `features.json` (F118 passes: true)
  1302→

  1306→- ✅ View moved from Jan 2024 to Dec 2024 with single fast drag

  1308→- ✅ No runtime errors related to momentum

# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Session: 2025-12-17 (STATE-ARCH-001 Planning Review) - COMPLETE ✅

### STATE-ARCH-001: High-Performance State Management - PLANNING COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Review and finalize the STATE-ARCH-001 task based on PDF research document, ensuring ALL performance recommendations are captured and @mms/toolkit usage is clearly documented.

**PDF Analyzed:**
- `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf` (11 pages)
- Key finding: GC is primary bottleneck, not rendering
- Recommends Dual-Store: Simulation State (TypedArrays) + Application State (external store)

**Task Updated: STATE-ARCH-001**

Comprehensive 8-phase implementation plan:

1. **Phase 1 - Simulation Store:** TypedArrays with Structure of Arrays (SoA) pattern
2. **Phase 2 - Spatial Index:** RBush R-tree + dirty flagging
3. **Phase 3 - UI State Bridge:** @mms/toolkit ONLY for UI state (NEVER Zustand)
4. **Phase 4 - Sync Loop Scheduler:** Fixed execution order, physics/render decoupled
5. **Phase 5 - Object Pooling & GC Elimination:** Closures, for loops, pre-compute
6. **Phase 6 - Mobile Optimizations:** Passive listeners, iOS limits (256MB/380MB)
7. **Phase 7 - Undo/Redo:** Command Pattern + Immer patches
8. **Phase 8 - Worker Layer:** Optional, OffscreenCanvas + SharedArrayBuffer

**Critical Decision: @mms/toolkit over Zustand**

PDF recommends Zustand, but this project uses **@mms/toolkit** exclusively:
- Documented in 3 places with "NUNCA Zustand" warning
- Clarifications added to phases explaining Simulation vs UI state separation
- @mms/toolkit is ONLY for UI state (<30fps, event-driven)
- Simulation State uses TypedArrays with direct access (60fps)

**Verification Completed:**
- All 11 PDF sections mapped to implementation phases
- All recommendations from Sections 2-9 covered
- Risk matrix mitigations included
- Performance targets defined (100k entities, 60fps, <100MB, <5ms queries)

**Files Changed:**
- `features.json` - STATE-ARCH-001 with 8 phases (+252 lines)
- `CLAUDE.md` - High-Performance Architecture guidelines (+35 lines)

**Status:** Planning complete. Task ready for implementation when prioritized.

---

## Session: 2025-12-17 (PERF-002 + BUG-009 Planning) - COMPLETE ✅

### PERF-002: Viewport Culling & Spatial Indexing - PLANNED
### BUG-009: Trackpad Zoom Performance - IDENTIFIED

**Objective:**
Deep research and planning for viewport culling optimization + Investigation of trackpad zoom performance issue on MacBook.

**Research Conducted:**
- Web searches: Three.js frustum culling, R3F viewport culling, RBush spatial indexing, deck.gl massive rendering, Figma WebGL architecture
- Read: `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf` (11 pages)
- Read: `docs/RESEARCH.md` (railway scheduling - not relevant)
- Read: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (current LOD implementation)

**Key Findings:**

1. **InstancedMesh Problem:** Three.js sends ALL instances to GPU regardless of viewport
   - 60k instances = 60k transforms uploaded every frame
   - No built-in per-instance frustum culling

2. **RBush Spatial Index:**
   - Bulk load 1M items ~1600ms (one-time)
   - Viewport query for 1000 results ~4ms
   - Perfect for viewport culling queries

3. **Culling Strategy:**
   - Reorder visible instances to front of buffer
   - Set `mesh.count = visibleCount` (only visible rendered)
   - No new allocations during pan/zoom

4. **Alternative Libraries:**
   - `@three.ez/instanced-mesh` (InstancedMesh2) - has BVH + per-instance culling
   - `three-mesh-bvh` - accelerated raycasting
   - `flatbush` - 5-8x faster than RBush for static data

**Documents Created:**
- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` - Comprehensive research consolidating all findings

**Task Created: PERF-002**

Added to features.json with 4 implementation phases:

1. **Phase 1 - Spatial Index:** RBush R-tree for task/entity lookup
2. **Phase 2 - Culled InstancedMesh:** Instance reordering + count manipulation
3. **Phase 3 - Layer Integration:** MesoLayer, MacroLayer viewport-aware
4. **Phase 4 - Validation:** Performance testing and profiling

**Dependency Update:**
- STATE-ARCH-001 now `blockedBy: "PERF-002"`
- PERF-002 is incremental improvement that can be done BEFORE full architecture migration
- Establishes viewport culling foundation that STATE-ARCH-001 will build upon

**Performance Targets:**
- Viewport query: <5ms for 1000 results
- Instance update: <1ms for 1000 visible
- FPS: 60fps constant during pan/zoom
- GPU instances: Only visible count (not all 60k)

**BUG-009 Investigation (Added):**
- User reported: Trackpad zoom has lag/stutter, but minimap navigation is smooth
- Diagnosis: This is INPUT HANDLING issue, not rendering (minimap proves rendering is fine)
- Root cause candidates:
  - ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION=250ms creates queued animations
  - ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT=50ms delays execution
  - animateZoomTo() starts new RAF instead of updating target
- Solution options:
  - Option A: Remove animation for trackpad (direct zoom like minimap)
  - Option B: Update target during animation
  - Option C: Use d3-zoom or @use-gesture/react (market solutions)

**Files Changed:**
- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` (NEW - comprehensive research)
- `features.json`:
  - Added PERF-002 with 5 phases (phase0 = BUG-009 fix first)
  - Added BUG-009 with detailed investigation
  - Updated STATE-ARCH-001 blockedBy: PERF-002
- `claude-progress.txt` (this file)

**Alignment Fix (Added):**
- PERF-002.Phase1 was duplicating STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 (both had RBush)
- Fix: PERF-002.Phase1 now defines ISpatialIndex INTERFACE only + placeholder
- STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 implements full RBush with TypedArrays
- No more duplicate work - tasks are now complementary

**Status:** Planning complete. Tasks aligned. Ready for implementation.

---

## Session: 2025-12-17 (STATE-ARCH-001 Planning) - COMPLETE ✅

### STATE-ARCH-001: High-Performance State Management Architecture - PLANNED

**Objective:**
Plan refactoring of state management architecture based on research document analyzing Figma, Google Maps, CesiumJS, and WebGL game engines.

**Research Document:**
- `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf`
- Key finding: GC (Garbage Collection) is the primary bottleneck, not rendering
- React Hooks and reactive stores inadequate for 60fps on mobile
- Recommends Dual-Store: Simulation State (TypedArrays) + Application State (external store)

**Task Created: STATE-ARCH-001**

Added comprehensive task to features.json with 5 implementation phases:

1. **Phase 1 - Foundation:** Create EntityStore with TypedArrays (Structure of Arrays pattern)
2. **Phase 2 - Spatial Index:** Implement RBush R-tree for viewport queries
3. **Phase 3 - UI Bridge:** Connect Engine State to @mms/toolkit (NOT Zustand as PDF suggests)
4. **Phase 4 - Object Pooling:** Eliminate GC pauses in animation loops
5. **Phase 5 - Undo/Redo:** Command Pattern + Immer patches

**CRITICAL DECISION: @mms/toolkit over Zustand**

The PDF recommends Zustand for UI state, but this project uses **@mms/toolkit** (packages/toolkit):
- Already implemented and integrated
- Uses Immer for immutability
- Uses useSyncExternalStore for synchronous updates
- Has slice-based architecture matching project needs

**Files Changed:**
- `features.json` - Added STATE-ARCH-001 task with detailed implementation plan
- `CLAUDE.md` - Added "High-Performance Architecture" section with guidelines

**Performance Targets:**
- 100,000+ entities renderable
- 60fps constant during pan/zoom
- <100MB memory for 100k entities
- <5ms viewport queries
- Zero perceptible GC pauses

**Next Steps:**
- Implementation will be phased over multiple sessions
- Start with Phase 1 (EntityStore) when prioritized

---

## Session: 2025-12-17 (UX Bug Fixes) - IN PROGRESS 🔄

### UX Bug Fix Sprint

**Context:**
User reported multiple UX bugs after features were marked complete. This session addresses all reported issues.

## Session: 2025-12-17 (Parallel Harness Setup) - COMPLETE ✅

### HARNESS-005: Parallel Execution Infrastructure - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Create infrastructure to run multiple features in parallel using git worktrees, enabling ~2.5x faster development.

**Research:**
- Studied [Anthropic's Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- Researched git worktree patterns for AI agent parallel development
- Analyzed file conflict potential for all 10 pending features

**Analysis of Pending Features (10 total):**

```
WAVE 1 (Can run in parallel - 3 groups):
├── Group A: F023 (Dependency lines) → MicroLayer.tsx [LOW risk]
├── Group B: F123, F025 (Minimap) → App.tsx [MEDIUM risk, sequential]
└── Group C: F016, F017 (Scroll modifiers) → InfiniteCanvas.tsx [HIGH risk, sequential]

WAVE 2 (After Wave 1 merge - 2 groups):
├── Group D: F027, F028 (Keyboard shortcuts) → InfiniteCanvas.tsx [HIGH risk, sequential]
└── Group E: F026 (Task details panel) → App.tsx [MEDIUM risk]

WAVE 3 (After Wave 2 merge - 1 group):
└── Group F: F021 (Keyboard task navigation) → Multiple files [HIGH risk]

WAVE 4 (After Wave 3 merge - 1 group):
└── Group G: F022 (ARIA labels) → Multiple files [LOW risk]
```

**Files Created:**
- `scripts/parallel-config.ts` - Wave/group configuration with conflict analysis
- `scripts/parallel-runner.ts` - Interactive parallel execution runner
- `docs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md` - Complete documentation

**Files Modified:**
- `.gitignore` - Added `.trees/` for worktree directories
- `features.json` - Added `parallelExecution` section with waves configuration

**Key Features of Parallel Runner:**
1. **Git worktree management** - Creates isolated working directories
2. **Terminal session spawning** - Opens separate Claude sessions per feature
3. **Isolation prompts** - Each session restricted to its feature scope
4. **Wave dependencies** - Prevents starting Wave N before Wave N-1 completes
5. **Merge helper** - Automates feature branch merging
6. **Status display** - Shows progress across all waves/groups
7. **macOS notifications** - Alerts for session events
8. **Cleanup command** - Removes all worktrees

**Speedup Calculation:**
- Sequential: 10 features = 10 sessions
- Parallel: 4 waves (2+2+1+1) = ~4 "effective sessions"
- Estimated speedup: **2.5x**

**Usage:**
```bash
# Show status
bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts status

# Execute Wave 1
bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts wave 1

# Merge completed feature
bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts merge F023
```

   - Ring border style for consistency

## Session: 2025-12-17 (TECH-DEBT-001 Implementation) - COMPLETE ✅

### TECH-DEBT-001: Migrate Camera State to @mms/toolkit State Machine - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Replace React useState/useRef with @mms/toolkit external store to solve stale closure problems in animation loops.

**Implementation:**

1. **Created viewport slice (`apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts`):**
   - ViewportSliceState interface with camera, canvasSize, momentum, animation, pan states
   - createSyncSlice with all reducers: setCamera, setMomentum, startAnimation, startPan, etc.
   - Cache policy: 'session' (resets when app closes)

2. **Created canvas machine (`apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts`):**
   - createMachine with viewport slice
   - Exports: useCanvas, useCanvasSelector, canvasActions, canvasStore
   - Context with userID: null (anonymous canvas)

3. **Migrated InfiniteCanvas.tsx:**
   - Replaced useState calls with useCanvasSelector for reactive state
   - Animation loops use canvasStore.getState() for synchronous access (no stale closures)
   - Pan/zoom handlers use canvasStore.dispatch(canvasActions.viewport.xxx())
   - Removed cameraPosRef workaround (no longer needed with external store)

**Errors Fixed:**
- `cache: { strategy: 'none' }` → `cache: { policy: 'session' }` (toolkit requires 'policy' field)
- `context: {}` → `context: { userID: null }` (toolkit requires userID in context)

**Files Created:**
- `apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts` (~235 lines)
- `apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts` (~55 lines)
- `apps/canvas/src/state/index.ts` (re-exports)

**Files Modified:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (full migration from useState to store)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ MACRO layer (0.44x): Heatmap renders correctly
- ✅ MESO layer (1.00x): Task blocks with row backgrounds
- ✅ MICRO layer (2.25x): Task cards with labels
- ✅ Zoom in/out buttons: Working (1.00x → 2.25x → 5.00x)
- ✅ LOD transitions: Smooth (MESO → MICRO → back)
- ✅ Reset View: Returns to initial state
- ✅ No console errors

**Benefits Achieved:**
- Animation loops always read current state via store.getState()
- No more stale closure issues in momentum/zoom animations
- Clean separation of concerns (state logic in slice, rendering in component)
- Foundation for future state slices (selection, hover, etc.)

## Session: 2025-12-16 (PERF-001 Implementation) - COMPLETE ✅

### PERF-001: Performance Monitor with Logging System - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Add real-time WebGL performance metrics tool with detailed logging for both visual and programmatic analysis.

**Implementation:**

1. **r3f-perf Integration:**
   - Installed r3f-perf 7.2.3 via `bun add r3f-perf`
   - Visual panel shows: GPU, CPU, FPS, draw calls, triangles, geometries, textures, shaders

2. **PerfMonitor Component (`apps/canvas/src/components/PerfMonitor.tsx`):**
   - ~400 lines of code with types, logging system, and visual panel
   - PerfLogger sub-component tracks metrics every frame
   - Circular buffer stores last 60s of performance data
   - Rolling averages calculated over 60-frame windows

3. **Global Console API:**
   - `window.__PERF_LOG__` - Access log buffer (last 60s)
   - `window.__PERF_EXPORT__()` - Export full log as array
   - `window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name)` - Start recording session
   - `window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__()` - Stop and get aggregated stats
   - `window.__PERF_SESSION_STATUS__()` - Check active session
   - `window.__PERF_CLEAR__()` - Clear log buffer

4. **Session Statistics:**
   - FPS: avg, min, max, p1 (worst 1%), p99 (best 99%)
   - MS: avg, max frame time
   - Frame counts: below 30fps, below 60fps
   - Duration and total frames

5. **UI Integration:**
   - Toggle visibility via 'P' key
   - "Perf" button in navbar
   - Panel positioned top-right
   - Runs in development mode by default

**Known Issue:**
- r3f-perf causes console spam with `expandShaderIncludes TypeError`
- Root cause: troika-three-utils 0.49.0 incompatible with Three.js 0.170+
- Fix exists in troika-three-utils 0.50.3+ but r3f-perf hasn't updated
- Tracked in: https://github.com/utsuboco/r3f-perf/issues/58
- Does NOT affect functionality - panel and logging work correctly

**Files Created/Modified:**
- `apps/canvas/src/components/PerfMonitor.tsx` (NEW)
- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` (perf state, toggle, event tracking)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (PerfMonitor props)
- `docs/PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md` (NEW - comprehensive documentation)

- Session API works: START_SESSION, SESSION_STATUS, STOP_SESSION
- Performance logging active even when panel hidden

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F116 - Performance Optimization) - COMPLETE ✅

### F116: Performance optimization (60fps) - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Ensure 60fps performance during pan/zoom with InstancedMesh optimizations and frustum culling.

**Analysis:**
Reviewed all three LOD layers to identify performance bottlenecks:

1. **MesoLayer (BEFORE):**
   - TaskBlocks: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   - ConflictGlows: InstancedMesh ✅ (already optimized)
   - RowBackgrounds: **50 individual meshes = 50 draw calls** ❌

2. **MicroLayer:** Already fully optimized
   - Frustum culling (filterVisibleTasks)
   - InstancedMesh for grid lines (2 draw calls)
   - InstancedMesh for row backgrounds
   - Lazy label rendering (max 50 labels)
   - Performance skip when opacity < 0.3

3. **MacroLayer:** Already optimized
   - InstancedMesh for heatmap cells
   - YearMarkers: ~11 individual meshes (acceptable)

**Solution:**
Converted MesoLayer RowBackgrounds from individual meshes to InstancedMesh:
- **Before:** 50 individual `<mesh>` elements = 50 draw calls
- **After:** 1 `<instancedMesh>` = 1 draw call
- **Improvement:** 98% reduction in draw calls for row backgrounds

**Files Changed:**

- ✅ LOD transitions smooth (no flickering)
- ✅ No console errors

**Performance Summary:**

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F122 - Fix elastic/jump drag bug) - COMPLETE ✅

### F122: Fix elastic/jump effect when dragging - COMPLETE

**Problem:**
Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.

**Root Cause Analysis:**
React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:
1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state
2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position
3. New drag starts from stale position → canvas "jumps" back

**Solution Implemented:**
1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef
2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state
3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:
   - `handleCameraInitialized`
   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)
   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)
   - `handlePinchZoom`
   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)
   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)
4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)
- `features.json` (F122 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Single drag pans correctly
- ✅ Drag during momentum - no jump/elastic effect
- ✅ Rapid drag-release-drag sequences (5 iterations) - smooth panning
- ✅ No console errors

### Harness Updates

**F117 Archived:**
- "Full visual parity with POC reference" no longer relevant
- Production app has surpassed POC in functionality and UX

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F120 + F121 - Double-click zoom + Scroll normalization) - COMPLETE ✅

### F120: Double-click to zoom in - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement double-click to zoom in 2x centered on click location.

**Implementation:**
- Already implemented in F119 (smooth zoom) session
- `handleDoubleClick` function in InfiniteCanvas.tsx (lines 1009-1074)
- Zooms 2x toward click point with 350ms cubicOut animation
- Respects MAX_ZOOM (5.0x) limit

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Double-click at 1.00x → 2.00x (2x zoom)
- ✅ Double-click at 2.00x → 4.00x (2x zoom)
- ✅ Double-click at 4.00x → 5.00x (clamped to MAX_ZOOM)
- ✅ Double-click at 5.00x stays at 5.00x (respects limit)
- ✅ Smooth animation with cubicOut easing
- ✅ No console errors

### F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Normalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.

**Implementation:**

1. **deltaMode Normalization:**
   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)
   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)
   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly

2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**
   - Track event frequency with timestamps
   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior
   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad
   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode

3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**
   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003
   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)

4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**
   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5
   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events

**Constants Added:**
```typescript
LINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line
PAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page
MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity
TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad
MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event
TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events
TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm
```

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (normalizeWheelDelta function, updated handleWheel)
- `features.json` (F120, F121 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Scroll down (deltaY=300) zooms out: 1.00x → 0.61x
- ✅ Scroll up (deltaY=-500) zooms in: 0.61x → 1.00x
- ✅ No console errors
- ✅ Smooth zoom animation

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE ✅

### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.

**Implementation:**

1. **Velocity Tracking:**
   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps
   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation
   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity

2. **Momentum Animation:**
   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples
   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity
   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down
   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units

3. **Integration:**
   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)
   - Cleanup animation on component unmount
   - Camera position clamped to world bounds during animation

**Constants Used:**
```typescript
MOMENTUM_FRICTION = 0.93     // Per-frame velocity multiplier
MOMENTUM_MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5  // Stop threshold
MOMENTUM_VELOCITY_SAMPLES = 5 // Samples to average
```

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (momentum implementation)
- `features.json` (F118 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ App loads at Jan 2024 (initial position)
- ✅ Fast drag moves view with momentum continuation
- ✅ View moved from Jan 2024 to Dec 2024 with single fast drag
- ✅ Momentum respects world bounds
- ✅ No runtime errors related to momentum

Confirmado! Em zoom 0.36x (MACRO), o **Timeline Header está completamente vazio** - não há nenhum label de data visível entre o navbar e o sidebar/canvas.

O problema está claro agora:
1. No modo MACRO (zoom < 0.5), a granularidade é "year"
2. Os labels de ano são gerados mas estão fora do viewport visível ou são filtrados

Vou analisar o código do TimelineHeader:
- Em zoom baixo, o `viewportLeftX` e `viewportRightX` cobrem uma área ENORME em world coordinates
- Os labels de ano são espaçados a cada 365 dias = 3650 pixels (at pxPerDay=10)
- Se a câmera estiver centrada no início, os labels podem estar fora do range de percentPosition

O problema provavelmente está na função `generateLabels()` ou no cálculo de `percentPosition`. Em zoom baixo:
- viewportWidth é muito grande
- O label de "2024" está em worldX=0 ou próximo
- Se viewportLeftX é negativo ou o cálculo de percent dá um valor fora do range -20% a 120%, o label é skipado

Vou verificar o código App.tsx para ver como viewportLeftX/Right são calculados e passados ao TimelineHeader.

content.thinking: O seletor não encontrou o elemento. Vou tentar uma abordagem diferente para encontrar o TimelineHeader.

Interessante! O label "2024" EXISTE no DOM, está em `left: 0%` com `transform: translateX(0%)`. Mas eu não vi o label no screenshot...

Olhando mais de perto:
- O label está em `left: 0%` - isso significa que está no canto esquerdo do TimelineHeader
- `transform: translateX(0%)` - não está centralizado no ponto, está alinhado à esquerda

E há um gradiente cobrindo o lado esquerdo:
```html
<div style="position: absolute; left: 0px; top: 0px; bottom: 0px; width: 30px; background: linear-gradient(to right, rgb(245, 248, 250), transparent); pointer-events: none;">
```

Esse gradiente de 30px de largura está **COBRINDO** o label "2024" que está em `left: 0%`!

Aqui está o problema:
1. Em zoom baixo, o percentPosition para "2024" é provavelmente muito baixo (perto de 0% ou negativo)
2. O código tem lógica para edge labels: se percentPosition < 5, usa `transformX = '0%'` (alinhado à esquerda)
3. Mas o gradiente fade de 30px cobre exatamente essa área!

Agora entendi o bug. Vou corrigir:
1. Mover o z-index dos labels acima dos gradientes, OU
2. Aumentar a área visível dos labels em edge cases, OU
3. Dar padding extra para os labels não ficarem cobertos pelos gradientesVou implementar a correção.

content.text:
Encontrei o problema! O label "2024" existe no DOM em `left: 0%`, mas está sendo **coberto pelo gradiente de fade** (30px de largura no lado esquerdo)!

O gradiente que faz o fade visual está sobrepondo o label. Vou corrigir isso agora.

content.text: O label "2024" aparece no accessibility tree. Vou tirar um screenshot para verificar se está visível:

content.thinking:
Hmm, ainda não vejo o label "2024" no screenshot! O accessibility tree diz que existe, mas visualmente não aparece. Preciso investigar mais.

Talvez o label esteja muito à esquerda e o background não seja suficiente para destacá-lo do resto. Deixe-me verificar o que está acontecendo com o DOM.

content.text: O label ainda não está visível no screenshot, apesar de existir no DOM. Vou investigar a posição exata:

content.thinking:
Interessante! O label "2024" está em:
- rect.x = 130 (ou seja, está exatamente na borda esquerda do TimelineHeader, que começa em x=130 após o sidebar de 130px)
- rect.y = 50.4 (dentro da área do TimelineHeader que está em y=50)
- width = 42.4px, height = 30.14px
- zIndex = 2, backgroundColor = rgb(245, 248, 250)

O label está dentro do container do TimelineHeader (que vai de x=130 a x=800). O label está em x=130, que é a posição correta.

Mas espera, no screenshot eu NÃO vejo o label "2024"! Olhando novamente o screenshot... Ele está lá mas talvez seja tão sutil que não percebi. Olhando com mais atenção...

Na verdade, o problema pode ser que o Timeline Header está colapsado visualmente ou o label está em uma posição que faz parecer invisível.

Olhando o screenshot novamente:
- O Navbar está no topo ("MMS Canvas", "Zoom: 0.44x", "MACRO", botões)
- Logo abaixo parece haver uma fina barra cinza
- Depois vem o sidebar "Trains" e o canvas

Mas eu não consigo ver "2024" em lugar nenhum! O label deveria estar logo abaixo do navbar, à esquerda, logo depois do sidebar.

Ah, entendi! O TimelineHeader tem `marginLeft: 130px` que o desloca para a direita do sidebar. O label está em `left: 0px` dentro do TimelineHeader, ou seja, está no x=130 da tela.

Olhando MUITO de perto no screenshot... entre o FPS monitor (top-left) e a área "Trains", deveria haver o TimelineHeader com "2024". Mas o FPS monitor parece estar sobrepondo essa área!
 
Investigando a sobreposição, confirmo que o StatsMonitor está bloqueando completamente o TimelineHeader. Com altura de 32px, o TimelineHeader deveria aparecer logo abaixo do Navbar, mas o monitor de desempenho o está cobrindo totalmente. Isso explica por que não consigo visualizar o label "2024" no local esperado.

content.text: Interessante! O label está em x=130, y=50 - exatamente onde o TimelineHeader deveria estar. Mas no screenshot ele não é visível. O problema pode ser o PerformancePanel (FPS monitor) que está em "top-left" cobrindo o TimelineHeader. Vou verificar:

content.thinking:
Encontrei o problema! O `PerformancePanel` está posicionado em `top: 50, left: 8` (linha 230) com `position: 'fixed'` e `zIndex: 10000`. Isso está sobrepondo o TimelineHeader que está em y=50.

O TimelineHeader está exatamente em y=50 (depois do Navbar de 50px) e o PerformancePanel está em top:50 com zIndex altíssimo (10000).

Vou mover o PerformancePanel para uma posição que não sobreponha o TimelineHeader. Como o Navbar tem ~50px e o TimelineHeader tem 32px, vou ajustar o top para ~90px ou mover para bottom-left.

content.text: Encontrei! O `PerformancePanel` está em `top: 50` com `zIndex: 10000`, sobrepondo exatamente o TimelineHeader! Vou corrigir movendo-o para `bottom-left`:

lines: {/* Performance panel - reliable FPS monitor using stats.js */}
+      {/* BUG-005: Moved to bottom-left to avoid overlapping TimelineHeader */}

content.thinking:
Agora vou investigar BUG-009. De acordo com features.json, o problema é que o zoom via trackpad tem lag/stutter mas o minimap funciona suave. As causas suspeitas são:1. ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION (250ms) cria animações enfileiradas durante pinch rápido
2. ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT (50ms) atrasa execução3. animateZoomTo() inicia novo requestAnimationFrame por evento de zoom em vez de atualizar targetVou ler o código relevante de handleWheel e animateZoomTo para entender a implementação atual.

Entendo o problema agora. O handleWheel atual:1. Detecta se é trackpad (evento rápido, 20ms threshold)2. Se for trackpad, acumula os deltas por ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT (50ms)3. Depois executa o zoom acumulado com animateZoomTo() que tem ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION (250ms)O problema é que CADA grupo de eventos acumulados inicia uma NOVA animação de 250ms. Se o usuário continua fazendo pinch, novas animações são iniciadas antes da anterior terminar, causando "queuing" de animações.A solução proposta é aplicar o zoom DIRETAMENTE para trackpad (sem animação), como o minimap faz.Vou ler o código do minimap para ver como ele aplica zoom:O minimap usa `setNavigationTarget` que eventualmente chama `animateZoomTo` também... mas com 350ms. Então talvez o problema não seja o minimap.Deixe-me reconsiderar. O problema pode ser:
1. A animação em si (250ms é longo demais para trackpad)
2. O accumulation timeout (50ms adiciona latência)
3. Cada evento trigger nova animação em vez de atualizar o targetA melhor solução para trackpad seria:
- **Opção A**: Remover animação para trackpad - aplicar zoom diretamente (como faz para o normal wheel scroll)
- **Opção B**: Fazer a animação "perseguir" o target em vez de iniciar nova animação

Opção A é mais simples. Vou implementá-la: quando detectar trackpad, aplicar zoom diretamente sem animação.

Vou ler a parte do handleWheel que detecta trackpad e ver como modificar.

content.text: Vou ler a implementação completa do handleWheel para entender melhor:

content.thinking:
Agora entendo a implementação. Quando é `e.ctrlKey` (pinch no trackpad ou Ctrl+scroll):

1. Acumula o delta2. Configura timeout de 50ms
3. Depois de 50ms, chama `executeAccumulatedZoom()` que faz `animateZoomTo()` com 250ms de animação

O problema é que CADA grupo de eventos gera uma animação de 250ms. Se o usuário faz pinch rápido continuamente, as animações se acumulam criando um lag percebido.A solução que vou implementar:**Para pinch no trackpad (ctrlKey=true), aplicar zoom DIRETAMENTE sem animação, similar ao handlePinchZoom que já faz isso.**

O código do `handlePinchZoom` (linhas 1262-1287) aplica zoom diretamente via `updateCameraPos()` e `onZoomChange()` - sem animação. Esse é o motivo do pinch em dispositivos touch ser suave.Vou modificar a seção de ctrlKey no handleWheel para:
1. Remover a acumulação e timeout2. Aplicar zoom diretamente (como handlePinchZoom faz)Isso será a "Option A" mencionada no features.json - aplicar zoom diretamente para trackpad.

content.text:
Agora entendo o problema! A implementação atual para pinch zoom (ctrlKey):
1. Acumula deltas por 50ms
2. Depois executa animação de 250ms

Isso causa lag porque cada grupo de eventos inicia uma nova animação. A solução é aplicar zoom **diretamente** (como `handlePinchZoom` faz para touch), sem animação.

Vou implementar a correção:

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below:
Analysis:
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. **Initial User Request**: The user asked to follow the Harness Protocol described in CLAUDE.md, continue with the next task, ignore the ./packages folder (handled by another team), execute the flow following the harness, and commit appropriately until all tasks are finalized and tested.

2. **Harness State Analysis**:
   - Read `claude-progress.txt` and `features.json` to understand current state
   - Found 53/59 features complete
   - Pending features (passes: false):
     - BUG-005 (priority 0) - Date values not visible at certain zoom levels
     - BUG-009 (priority 0) - Trackpad zoom performance issue
     - PERF-002 (priority 0) - Viewport Culling & Spatial Indexing
     - STATE-ARCH-001 (priority 1) - High-Performance State Management (blockedBy: PERF-002)

3. **BUG-005 Investigation**:
   - Started with BUG-005 as it seemed simpler
   - Navigated to localhost:5173 via Playwright MCP
   - Zoomed out to MACRO mode (0.44x zoom)
   - Took screenshots and discovered the Timeline Header labels were not visible
   - Used JavaScript evaluation to find the label "2024" existed in DOM at `left: 0%`
   - Root cause identified as twofold:
     1. TimelineHeader labels had no z-index, so gradient fade overlays covered them
     2. PerformancePanel (StatsMonitor) was positioned at `top: 50` with `zIndex: 10000`, overlapping the TimelineHeader

4. **BUG-005 Fix**:
   - Modified `TimelineHeader.tsx`:
     - Added `zIndex: 2` to labels
     - Added `backgroundColor: '#f5f8fa'` to labels
     - Reduced gradient overlay width from 30px to 20px
     - Set gradient `zIndex: 1`
   - Modified `App.tsx`:
     - Changed PerformancePanel position from `"top-left"` to `"bottom-left"`
   - Updated `features.json` to mark BUG-005 as passes: true
   - Committed changes

5. **BUG-009 Investigation Started**:
   - Started investigating trackpad zoom performance issue
   - Read the handleWheel and animateZoomTo code in InfiniteCanvas.tsx

content.thinking:
Now I can see the current implementation. The issue is clear:

1. `handleWheel` with ctrlKey (trackpad pinch) uses accumulation + timeout + animation:
   - Accumulates delta (line 1224)
   - Sets a 50ms timeout (line 1234)
   - Then calls `executeAccumulatedZoom()` which triggers a 250ms animation

2. `handlePinchZoom` (for touch devices) applies zoom DIRECTLY:
   - Calculates new zoom (line 1265)
   - Updates camera directly with `updateCameraPos()` (line 1282)
   - Calls `onZoomChange()` directly (line 1283)
   - NO animation, NO accumulation

The fix for BUG-009 is to make the trackpad pinch (ctrlKey branch) behave like touch pinch - apply zoom directly without animation.

Let me also read the executeAccumulatedZoom function to understand it better and see what calculations I need to replicate:

Now I understand the full picture. The fix is to modify the `handleWheel` function's ctrlKey branch to apply zoom **directly** (like `handlePinchZoom`), bypassing the accumulation timeout and animation.

lines: // Ctrl+scroll = ZOOM (pinch gesture on trackpad sends ctrlKey=true)
+      // BUG-009: Apply zoom DIRECTLY for trackpad pinch (no animation delay)

-        // Accumulate normalized delta (positive = zoom out, negative = zoom in)
+        // Skip if delta is too small
+        // Calculate zoom factor from normalized delta
+        // Negative delta = zoom in, positive delta = zoom out
+        const newZoom = Math.max(effectiveMinZoom, Math.min(MAX_ZOOM, zoom * zoomFactor));
-        // Set timeout to execute accumulated zoom
+        // Skip if zoom change is negligible
+        // Cursor position relative to canvas center
+        // Calculate new camera position to keep cursor point fixed in world space
+        // Apply directly (no animation) - same as handlePinchZoom for responsive feel

The code changes compile and work correctly. Since BUG-009 is specifically about trackpad pinch-to-zoom performance (which cannot be directly simulated via Playwright button clicks), the key verification is:

1. ✅ Code compiles without TypeScript errors
2. ✅ Zoom functionality still works (button zoom test passed)
3. ✅ No console errors
4. ✅ LOD transitions work correctly
5. ✅ FPS remains excellent

The fix removes the 50ms accumulation timeout + 250ms animation for trackpad pinch zoom, replacing it with direct zoom application. This should eliminate the perceived lag on trackpad devices.
